raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command that extracts recursively and outputs to stdout? Probably use tar -xzO? But recursively unzip files inside tar? Maybe use tar -xzf and grep. We want to search for "special" in the extracted content. So: tar -xzf /system/folder2.tar.gz | grep -i special? But tar outputs files to disk. To output to stdout, use tar -xOzf? That outputs each file content to stdout but not directory structure. For recursive, we can use tar -xOzf and pipe to grep. But need to search within files. Use tar -xOzf ... | grep -i special? But that will search across all file contents concatenated. Maybe use tar -xOzf ... | grep -i special. Provide command.

Use tar -xOzf ... | grep -i special? But that will search across all file contents concatenated. Maybe use tar -xOzf ... | grep -i special. Provide command.
